title: Concurrent Engineering Workshop
description: 4-day workshop on space mission design using the Concurrent Design Facility (CDF) methodology and the COMET tool. Students work in teams to produce a complete mission design concept. Held annually in September at ESEC-Galaxia.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: CubeSat Concurrent Engineering Workshop
description: 4-day hands-on workshop covering CubeSat mission design using concurrent engineering methods, with a visit to the AIV facility. Held annually in February/March. Open to BSc 3rd year and above.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Spacecraft Testing Workshop
description: 5-day workshop providing hands-on experience in cleanroom hardware testing and environmental testing procedures. Students work directly with spacecraft test equipment. Held annually in June.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Debris Training Course
description: 5-day course covering the space debris environment, mitigation strategies, operational procedures, and the challenges of large constellations. Held annually in September. Targeted at MSc/PhD level.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Robotics Workshop (ExoMy Rover)
description: 4-day hands-on workshop using ROS2, Python, and machine learning on the 3D-printed ExoMy rover. Two editions per year. Held at ESTEC, Noordwijk, Netherlands. Open from BSc 2nd year.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Earth Observation Remote Sensing Workshop
description: 5-day workshop on Sentinel and Copernicus satellite data, EO applications, and remote sensing techniques. Held annually in July. Targeted at MSc/PhD students.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Navigation & Galileo Training Course
description: 5-day course on GNSS, Galileo, and LEO-PNT systems with a facility tour. Held annually in June. Focuses on satellite navigation theory and current European navigation infrastructure.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Satellite Communications Training Course
description: 5-day course covering satellite communication systems, antennas, and link budgets, including a tour of the ESEC-Redu ground station. Open to BSc/MSc/PhD students.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Product Assurance (Mission Success) Course
description: 5-day course on product assurance principles and ECSS standards in space projects, held annually in November. Open to MSc/PhD engineering students and early-career professionals.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Systems Engineering Course
description: 4-day course on systems engineering for space missions: requirements, architecture, and trade-off analysis. Held annually in May. Ideal for students pursuing systems engineering careers.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Weather Training Course
description: 5-day course on solar activity, space weather forecasting, and its impact on space systems, with visits to ESEC-Redu and the Brussels Space Pole. Held annually in March.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Law Training Course
description: 5-day course on international space law, the Outer Space Treaty, and legal frameworks governing space activities. Open to students of any field including law, social sciences, and policy.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Standards / ECSS Course
description: 5-day course dedicated to ECSS (European Cooperation for Space Standardization) standards used in European space projects. Held annually in June at ESEC-Galaxia.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Technology Transfer, Application & Innovation Workshop
description: 4-day workshop on space technology spinoffs, business case development, and technology transfer pathways. Open to BSc 3rd year and above, any field. Held annually in January.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Life Support Systems 'Space for Earth' Workshop
description: Blended format: Week 1 onsite at ESEC-Galaxia, Week 2 online. Covers MELiSSA closed-loop life support, bioregenerative systems, and space-to-Earth business plan development. Held annually in March.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Access to Space Training Course
description: 5-day course on European launch vehicles, ESA space transportation policy, and the business of launch. No prior space knowledge required; open to any subject. Held annually in October.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Policy in Action Course
description: 5-day course on ESA history, space policy, governance, and a mock Ministerial Council exercise. Primarily for social sciences, law, and economics students but open to all. Held annually in July.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Human Space Physiology Course
description: 5-day course on the physiological effects of microgravity, space medicine, the ISS, and life sciences in space — with the opportunity to meet ESA astronauts. Held annually in December.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: CubeSat Summer School (Hands-On Training Week)
description: Approximately 2-week blended programme: Week 1 hands-on at ESEC-Galaxia with real ESAT CubeSat hardware, Week 2 online. Covers full CubeSat development lifecycle. Held annually in summer.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Gravity Research for Future Space Exploration Summer School
description: 2-week blended summer school on microgravity research, experiment proposal development, and future exploration missions in collaboration with ELGRA. Held annually in summer. Targeted at MSc/PhD.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Ladybird Guide to Spacecraft Communications
description: 5-day training inspired by the popular ESA Ladybird book, covering spacecraft communication fundamentals with ESOC mission simulations and real mission case studies.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Space Security / Cybersecurity in Space Course
description: 5-day course on space system cybersecurity threats, defences, and governance, with a tour of ESEC-Redu. For engineering and computer science students at BSc/MSc/PhD level.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: Rocketry Training Programme
description: ESA Academy rocketry programme for student rocket teams. Currently closed — the next cycle has not yet been announced. Open to BSc/MSc/PhD students in rocket team organisations.
deadline_notes: Currently closed. Next cycle to be announced.
funding_details: Accommodation + meals + up to €350 travel reimbursement
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Cooperating States

title: REXUS/BEXUS — Rocket and Balloon Experiments for University Students
description: Annual programme allowing student teams to design and fly experiments on sounding rockets (REXUS) and stratospheric balloons (BEXUS). Call opens in summer; BEXUS launches in October and REXUS in March. Next cycle expected to open Summer 2026.
deadline_notes: Next cycle expected to open Summer 2026

title: Fly Your Satellite!
description: Multi-year ESA Academy programme providing end-to-end CubeSat mission support from design through launch on an ESA mission. Teams receive hands-on support from ESA engineers. Next cycle expected 2027.

title: Fly Your Satellite! Design Booster
description: Shorter variant of the Fly Your Satellite! programme focused on CubeSat design support without a launch opportunity. Teams receive expert mentoring from ESA engineers. Next cycle expected May 2026.
deadline_notes: Next cycle expected to open May 2026

title: Test Opportunities — ESA Facilities Access
description: Access to ESA testing facilities for student hardware, covering thermal-vacuum, vibration, EMC, and other environmental tests. Current cycle: TBD. Teams must apply with flight-ready or near-flight hardware.

title: ESA Academy Experiments Programme
description: Access to parabolic flight campaigns, orbital robotics lab, and other ESA research platforms for student experiment teams. Next cycle expected Q3 2026.

title: ESA Academy Academic Scholarship
description: Covers full MSc tuition fees at selected Bologna-compliant European space Master programmes. Programme is currently on hold under evaluation. Applied via university, not individually.
funding_details: Full MSc tuition fees covered
eligibility_countries: ESA Member States and Associate Members

title: ESA Academy Short Course Scholarship
description: Up to 5 scholarships per course for external summer schools and short courses selected by ESA Academy, including Alpbach, ISU SSP, and VKI. Apply via the course organiser directly.
location: Various (Alpbach, ISU host university, VKI Belgium, etc.)
funding_details: Covers course fees for external summer schools

title: ESA Academy Conference Student Sponsorship
description: Up to €750 covering travel, accommodation, and registration fees for students with an accepted contribution (talk or poster) at an ESA-sponsored conference. Eligible conferences include IAC, EUCASS, COSPAR, EPSC, SGC, and others.
deadline_notes: Recurring per conference — check ESA Academy page for current open calls
funding_details: Up to €750 travel + accommodation + registration

title: ESA Academy EuRoC Team Sponsorship
description: Up to €1,000 travel and accommodation support for student teams competing at the European Rocketry Challenge (EuRoC). New from 2025, awarded annually in October.
funding_details: Up to €1,000 travel + accommodation

title: EuRoC — European Rocketry Challenge
description: Annual European student rocketry competition held in October at Constância, Portugal. Teams design, build, and launch rockets competing in multiple categories. ESA Academy sponsorship of up to €1,000 available.

title: ERC — European Rover Challenge
description: Annual Mars rover design, build, and operate competition held in August-September near Kraków, Poland. Teams must be at least 75% students. Registration opens February-March.

title: Student Aerospace Challenge
description: Annual interdisciplinary competition where student teams design a suborbital vehicle distributed across work packages. Apply in October/November. ESA Grand Prix winner presents at an international space conference.

title: CanSat Competition
description: ESA-coordinated can-sized satellite competition for students aged 14-19, with national competitions held each spring in 25+ countries. Teams design, build, and launch a functional satellite inside a soda can.

title: Alpbach Summer School
description: Prestigious 10-day summer school in the Austrian Alps where 60 international students design a complete space mission in interdisciplinary teams. ESA contributes €500 of the €950 fee; national agencies often cover the rest. ESA Short Course Scholarship eligible.
funding_details: ESA contributes €500 of €950 fee; national agencies often cover remainder

title: ISU Space Studies Programme (SSP)
description: 9-week intensive international summer programme hosted by a different university each year. Covers all aspects of space: engineering, science, policy, management, and law. ESA Short Course Scholarship available.
funding_details: ESA Academy Short Course Scholarship available (partial)

title: VKI Lecture Series
organisation: von Kármán Institute for Fluid Dynamics (VKI)
description: Intensive short courses at the von Kármán Institute in Belgium covering aerodynamics, fluid dynamics, and propulsion. Several courses per year. ESA Short Course Scholarship eligible.
funding_details: ESA Academy Short Course Scholarship eligible

title: DLR Summer School on Space Propulsion
description: Annual 10-day fully funded summer school on space propulsion at DLR Lampoldshausen, Germany, running since 2011. Covers chemical, electric, and hybrid propulsion. Application deadline typically May.
funding_details: Fully funded by DLR (travel + accommodation)

title: DLR International Space Weather Camp (ISWC)
description: 4-week fully funded bilateral camp split between Germany (DLR) and the USA (University of Alabama Huntsville), with 8 German and 8 US students. Covers space weather physics and applications. Held annually in July-August.
funding_details: Fully funded by DLR (travel + accommodation)

title: PiNa — Pico and Nano-Satellite Workshop
description: Biennial international workshop on pico- and nano-satellite technology, alternating between Universität Würzburg and TU Berlin. Abstract submission required. ~€50 registration fee. Running since 2007.

title: BVSR Annual Conference
description: Annual student space conference for BVSR member clubs and guests, held each May in Germany. The 2026 edition is hosted by TUDSaT in Darmstadt, May 14–17, 2026.

title: NORSTEC Summit
description: Annual student-industry space conference in Norway bringing together student teams, industry, and agencies. The 2026 edition was held at Trondheim Spektrum on March 12, 2026.

title: IAA Small Satellite Symposium
description: Biennial small satellite symposium hosted by DLR in Berlin, one of the world's top three conferences in the field. Students can present papers and attend as delegates.

title: ESA Symposium on Rocket and Balloon Programmes
description: Biennial ESA symposium on sounding rocket and scientific balloon programmes. The 27th edition will be hosted in the Tromsø area, Norway in 2026. Student paper submissions are welcome.

title: ActInSpace Hackathon
description: Annual space tech innovation hackathon: local hackathons January 30-31, with international finals in Bordeaux in April. Teams solve real space industry challenges and develop business concepts from space assets.
location: Local events worldwide + Finals in Bordeaux, France

title: NASA Space Apps Challenge
description: Annual 48-hour global hackathon held in October across 550+ cities. The Oslo edition is hosted by Tekna Space and the Norwegian Space Cluster. Teams solve NASA-proposed open-data challenges.

title: Tekna Space Hackathon Bodø
description: Annual 3-day space debris hackathon held in Bodø, Norway. Travel and accommodation covered up to NOK 5,000. The prize is a KSAT summer internship — one of Norway's most valuable student space prizes.
funding_details: Travel + accommodation up to NOK 5,000 (≈ €430). Prize: KSAT summer internship.

title: Andøya Space Hackathon
organisation: Andøya Space / NORSTEC / KSAT / ESA Phi-Lab Norway
description: Annual 3-day autumn hackathon on Andøya island, Norway, focused on satellite data and Arctic challenges. Partners include Andøya Space, NORSTEC, KSAT, and ESA Phi-Lab Norway.

title: CASSINI Hackathon & Mentoring
description: Annual EU hackathon running simultaneously in 10 European countries each autumn. Local winners advance to the EU final; top 3 teams receive 6 months of expert mentoring. Focuses on space-based solutions for societal challenges.
funding_details: Top 3 teams receive 6-month expert mentoring programme

title: Starlab Space Mission Hackathon
organisation: University of Zurich / BVSR / ESA BIC Switzerland
description: Annual space mission concept design hackathon held in November in Zurich/Dübendorf, Switzerland. Partners include UZH, BVSR, and ESA BIC Switzerland.

title: UNIVERSEH Space Hackathon
description: Annual online and regional finals hackathon organised by the UNIVERSEH European university alliance, focusing on ecosystem engineering and space biospheres across French, German, Swedish, Polish, and Romanian institutions.
eligibility_countries: UNIVERSEH alliance countries (France, Germany, Sweden, Poland, Romania)

title: Copernicus Hackathon
description: Annual EU/ESA hackathon using Copernicus Earth observation data, held across multiple European cities. Teams develop solutions to environmental and societal challenges using satellite data.

title: StarTech BIP — Space Entrepreneurship: From Idea to Venture
description: Erasmus+-funded Blended Intensive Programme on space entrepreneurship and startup pitching, run by a University of Rome-led alliance. Annual summer programme with Erasmus+ travel grant available.
funding_details: Erasmus+ mobility grant (amount depends on country pair)

title: SEA-EU BIP — Marine & Satellite Data Literacy (Bodø)
description: Erasmus+-funded blended intensive programme on marine and satellite data literacy, hosted by Nord University in Bodø, Norway. Annual June-July programme with virtual component plus 5-day physical stay in Bodø.
funding_details: Erasmus+ mobility grant

title: ENHANCE Alliance BIPs
description: Annual call for Erasmus+-funded Blended Intensive Programmes across ENHANCE alliance universities, including space-related topics. Apply via home institution. Erasmus+ travel grant included.
funding_details: Erasmus+ mobility grant

title: UNIVERSEH Alliance BIPs
description: Space-focused Erasmus+-funded Blended Intensive Programmes across French, German, Swedish, Polish, and Romanian UNIVERSEH alliance universities. Annual call. Erasmus+ travel grant included.
funding_details: Erasmus+ mobility grant

title: NORSTEC — Norwegian Space Technology Collective
description: Founded 2024 in Trondheim, NORSTEC is the umbrella organisation for all 10 Norwegian student space organisations with 500+ members. Runs the Northstar Incubator, Pathway Rideshare, Skyline Launchpad, and Spacepodden podcast.

title: BVSR — Bundesverband studentischer Raumfahrt
description: German umbrella association for student space clubs with 19 full members and 2 Austrian correspondent members. Supports clubs with advocacy, networking, the ODIN design competition, and an annual national conference.

title: SGAC — Space Generation Advisory Council
description: Global network representing students and young space professionals to the UN, ESA, and industry, with chapters in every ESA member state. Organises the Space Generation Congress (SGC) co-located with the International Astronautical Congress (IAC).
location: Global (chapters in all ESA member states)

title: EUROAVIA — European Association of Aerospace Students
description: Founded in 1959, EUROAVIA connects 32+ local student groups across 16+ European countries with 1,300+ members. Organises events, technical workshops, and study trips across Europe.

title: SEDS Europe / UKSEDS
description: European chapters of Students for Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS), a global grassroots student space organisation. UKSEDS is the active UK chapter with events and projects for UK space students.

title: Women in Aerospace Europe (WIA-E)
description: Pan-European network supporting women in aerospace through mentoring, networking, and career development programmes. Organises an annual WIA-E conference and regional events across Europe.
